title: iris pumila subsp. taurica
intro: Iris pumila subsp. taurica is a subspecies of the Dwarf Iris plant, known for its delicate purple flowers and compact growth habit. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from pain and swelling. In herbal preparations, Iris pumila subsp. taurica is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
